task_completion.visual_evidence: The Avare application interface is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The main Map screen of the Avare app is accessed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main Map screen is visible, showing a map and dashboard data field slots (typically at the top or bottom of the screen).

task_completion.description: The data field selection menu is opened to configure a slot.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog or menu listing various data field options is visible on the screen, typically triggered by double-tapping a data field slot.

task_completion.description: The 'Fuel Tank Switch Timer' option is located in the data field list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Fuel Tank Switch Timer' is visible within the data field selection menu.

task_completion.description: The Fuel Tank Switch Timer is successfully displayed in a data field slot on the main Map screen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: One of the data field slots on the main Map screen displays the Fuel Tank Switch Timer, which appears as a countdown value in MM.SS format (e.g., '30.00') or labels like 'Fuel' or 'Timer'.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to configure the timer.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show GUI elements like menus and dialogs being interacted with, rather than terminal windows or text editors.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine Avare application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application interface matches the standard Avare app layout, and is not a mock-up or a different application.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI by directly editing configuration files.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of text editors or terminal commands modifying Avare's internal preferences or XML files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Avare is an aviation GPS app (com.ds.avare). To configure the Fuel Tank Switch Timer, the user typically double-taps a display field on the Map screen to open a selection list, then selects 'Fuel Tank Switch Timer'. Once configured, the data field slot on the main Map screen will display the timer, which defaults to a countdown value in MM.SS format (e.g., '30.00' for 30 minutes), or may show labels like 'Fuel' or 'Timer'. When the countdown reaches zero, a dialog indicating 'Switch Fuel Tanks' is displayed.
